parsed_review.summary: CP00-801 is a descriptor-only, no-runtime, no-real-data closeout pack for RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening (RP26.P04.M06.S03-RP26.P05.M02.S14, 150 units, risk C). The pack-level claim is supported and contains no runtime/enterprise-trust overclaim: the manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, and the production_ready_flag is an explicitly descriptor-verified flag only. Verification through Read/Grep/Glob confirms: (1) Identity/coverage agree across the registry binding, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json (same range, unit_count=150, ledger SHA 13c7ed65...); the coverage validator and model.test.js enforce deliverable/phase/micro/title distributions matching the review baseline (ui:43, claude_review:13, security_audit:14, implementation:41, fixture:25, test:8, hermes_evidence:6). (2) Every case-set row is built descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false with all tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret/permission/audit leak flags false, and freezeResult merges the full ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION into every result; validators.js independently rejects any row that opens identity runtime, creates/allows cross-tenant routes, generates/exposes secrets, evaluates/writes permission decisions, writes audit events, or claims enterprise trust. (3) Authority boundary holds: descriptor.authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. (4) Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false), not product runtime. (5) The Claude packet is read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, with invalid_review_blockers enumerated. (6) The committed contract promotes CP00-801 to latest_pack/latest_projection with prior packs CP788-CP800 preserved as historical and top-level validation.valid=true. Local-validation evidence (validation-report.json, readiness-report.json) is clean: prompt within size, no full diff/source embedded, read_tools_used, schema present, no forbidden tools, auth preflight passed with well-formed JSON; all expected npm validate scripts exist in package.json. No prior review receipts require CP178+ rejection (valid_review_receipts empty). One non-blocking P3: the downstream closeout artifacts are not yet present, which is expected at this pre-review stage. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer; final human approval for enterprise trust/runtime opening is correctly retained outside this receipt.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts not yet generated (expected at pre-review stage)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json currently exists in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-801/ (artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-801/ likewise has no raw-output.json or review-receipt.json yet). This is consistent with a mid-closeout pack whose pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' with valid_review_receipts empty: claude-review-result.json/adjudication.md are produced by/after this very review, and command-evidence.json/construction-inspection.json are produced at closeout finalization. The comparison pack CP00-800 (already closed) contains all five artifacts, confirming this is sequencing rather than a defect. Because these are downstream of the review, the 'adjudication' and 'inspection' inputs named in the review focus cannot be independently verified from within this receipt.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this review. Ensure npm run closeout-pack:validate (which enforces required_pack_artifacts) is run and passes before the final CP00-801 closeout commit, so command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are present and adjudicated prior to marking the pack closed.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected/non-blocking. Artifacts are generated by this review plus the closeout finalization step and enforced by the closeout-pack:validate gate before commit; does not block pack or goal closeout.
